user_message: Need help with Wide World Importers -- run the hygiene check. I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 12:08 EST
Subject: Re: partner engagement Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the partner engagement proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 15:35 EST
Subject: Re: Re: partner engagement Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with partner engagement. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 11:44 EST
Subject: partner engagement - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with partner engagement. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-02-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Kevin (Wide World Importers)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:08:00] Kevin (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:20:00] Kevin (Wide World Importers): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] Kevin (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
